mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and signals reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.0, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields negative forward 60-day returns in more than 60% of observed quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, triggering short-term risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in over 58% of instances following an INDPRO decline, or the mechanism fails to predict direction during post-shock recovery periods specifically.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, monetary policy enters an accommodative extreme that compresses equity discount rates and fuels liquidity-driven buying, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward 60-day returns in over 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect immediate construction sector activity and broader economic confidence, which translates into short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months following a housing starts increase, or the Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 95 percent of its 2017 baseline, manufacturing weakness signals late-cycle deterioration that outweighs monetary support, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where INDPRO drops below 95 during late-cycle expansions, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 45% in out-of-sample tests.
